title: Zero dropout policy draws praise
desc: Thailand's zero dropout policy is gaining momentum as it works to return children to classrooms while preventing new losses.

title: BBL maintains loan growth target amid global volatility
desc: Bangkok Bank (BBL) expects crude oil prices to remain elevated despite an eventual end to the war in the Middle East, while encouraging local businesses to accelerate their transition to alternative energy.

title: Chadchart  eyes another term in office
desc: Bangkok governor Chadchart Sittipunt plans to announce his bid for re-election as an independent before his term ends next month.

title: Metthier rolls out robots at Suvarnabhumi
desc: Metthier, a subsidiary of SET-listed SKY ICT, is set to deploy three humanoid robots at Suvarnabhumi Airport by the end of this year to assist passengers with the pre-boarding process.

title: OR launches self-service fuel pilot
desc: PTT Oil and Retail Business Plc (OR), the oil trading arm of PTT Group, is conducting a market test of its self-service fuel system as part of preparations for potential labour shortages and rising minimum wages.

title: Petrol station night curfew considered for motorists
desc: If the government proceeds with a plan to close petrol stations at night from this week, the move is expected to heighten public awareness of volatile oil supplies and rising prices, which are stoking fears of economic strain, according to an energy industry executive.

title: New plan to lower oil price
desc: Energy Minister Akanat Promphan has signalled that a second reduction in refining margins of more than 2 baht per litre could be implemented this week, raising expectations of relief at the pump.

title: Leadership shake-up for opposition People's Party
desc: The People's Party (PP) is set to undergo a significant leadership reshuffle, with Veerayooth Kanchoochat, currently deputy leader, positioned to take over from party leader Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut, sources say.

title: Spectre of stagflation haunts Thailand
desc: Thailand's economy is increasingly seen as fragile now that the Songkran holiday has come to an end, as price freezes on energy and other products are discontinued.

title: PM, key ministers head North to ramp up pollution fight
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul will travel to Chiang Mai today with a team of ministers to step up efforts to tackle PM2.5 pollution in the North.

title: Heat in 'danger' zone for 19 days
desc: Bangkok has sweltered through 19 straight days of dangerous heat index levels, with extreme heat and humidity pushing conditions into a high-risk zone for public health.

title: Oman ties help govt navigate Mideast crisis
desc: Thailand's engagement with Oman has intensified following rising maritime insecurity in the Strait of Hormuz after Foreign Affairs Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow's visit to Muscat last week, where talks focused on shipping safety, energy cooperation and regional coordination.

title: Pattaya City  pushes Koh  Lan overhaul
desc: Pattaya City is advancing infrastructure and environmental projects on Koh Lan, aiming to transform the island into a sustainable tourism spot.

title: Senate panel proposes VAT hike to curb debt
desc: The Senate's Economic, Monetary and Fiscal Affairs Committee has unveiled a broad tax reform proposal, including a hike in value-added tax (VAT), to address rising public debt, which is projected to approach or exceed legal limits in the coming years.

title: Thai firms exit Cambodia amid tensions
desc: Amid lingering resentment among some Cambodian customers, a number of Thai business operators have temporarily halted operations in the neighbouring country's market.

title: Measures seek to bolster SMEs' bidding clout
desc: The Comptroller General's Department is preparing to roll out measures designed to strengthen small and medium-sized enterprises' (SMEs) competitiveness against large businesses in government procurement bidding.

title: Brother leans on 'efficiency' strategy in tough market
desc: Brother Commercial (Thailand) Ltd is accelerating its shift towards solution-led offerings to help businesses and entrepreneurs unlock new income streams and improve efficiency, while tightening costs to keep prices stable amid rising cost pressures.

title: HPE unveils Thai IT infrastructure tie-up
desc: VST ECS (Thailand) has joined forces with Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) to introduce VST ECS Co-Location As-a-Service, a comprehensive IT infrastructure platform that brings infrastructure services together within a single platform.

title: Kraftwerk brings their 'Multimedia Tour' to Thailand
desc: Electronic music pioneers Kraftwerk will bring their acclaimed "Kraftwerk Multimedia Tour 2026" to Bangkok, performing at the Queen Sirikit National Convention Center, Ratchadaphisek Road, on May 10 at 7.30pm.

title: A new stage for Asia
desc: Bangkok is about to take on a new kind of spotlight. The Thai capital has been officially announced as the host city for the inaugural Eurovision Song Contest Asia 2026, marking the first time the long-running global music phenomenon expands into the region.

title: Vir Das brings his 'Hey Stranger' comedy tour to Bangkok
desc: Emmy award-winning and global comedy sensation Vir Das will make a comeback with his new show "Hey Stranger" at KBank Siam Pic-Ganesha, 7th floor of Siam Square One, Rama I Road, on May 17 at 7pm.

title: Thai wetlands in focus at Siam Society talk
desc: The Natural History Section of Siam Society is holding a panel discussion on "Wiang Nong Lom: The Significance Of Wetlands" at Siam Society, Asok Road, on April 25 from 9.30am to noon.

title: Bangkok swelters in 'danger' zone for 19 straight days
desc: Bangkok has sweltered through 19 straight days of dangerous heat index levels, with extreme heat and humidity pushing conditions into a high-risk zone for public health.

title: Anutin to visit Chiang Mai to tackle forest-fire smog
desc: Prime Minister and Interior Minister Anutin Charnvirakul will lead a multi‑agency delegation to Chiang Mai on Monday to follow up on efforts to address forest fires, haze and PM2.5 pollution cloaking the upper North.

title: Nearly 100 workers flee massive Ayutthaya factory fire
desc: Nearly 100 workers flee massive Ayutthaya factory fire Legacy A massive fire broke out at an electronics materials factory in Ayutthaya today, April 19, sending nearly 100 workers out of the building and prompting checks for any impact on nearby communities . The fire started at about 1.30pm inside a Chinese-owned factory in the Rojana Industrial Park, Khan Ham subdistrict, Uthai district, Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya. One warehouse was damaged in the fire . Nearly 100 employees were working at the time, most of them Myanmar workers.

title: Police open leaking suitcase in Pathum Thani, find puppy remains
desc: Police open leaking suitcase in Pathum Thani, find puppy remains Legacy Residents in Pathum Thani were alarmed after a black suitcase left beside a house began leaking fluid , giving off a strong smell and attracting maggots , prompting police to inspect it on April 17.

title: Five stranded tourists rescued from Phetchaburi mountain
desc: Five tourists were rescued after becoming stuck on a mountain in Phetchaburi last night, April 18, with one 21 year old man later taken to the hospital after feeling faint during the descent .

title: 泰国拟上调增值税至10%以应对长期财政赤字
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月19日，泰国参议院经济、金融与财政委员会已完成税制改革研究报告，计划于4月21日提交参议院审议并转交内阁进一步决策。其中，将增值税由7%上调至10%的提议，成为本轮改革的核心焦点。

title: Two die in Bang Na townhouse blaze, cause under investigation
desc: Two die in Bang Na townhouse blaze, cause under investigation Legacy A fire tore through a townhouse at a luxury housing project on Udom Suk Road in Bang Na , Bangkok, early on Sunday, killing two people and injuring two others.

title: 骑手一念警觉破大案，6名韩国人涉网赌被捕！
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月19日，泰国警方在曼谷展开突击行动，成功破获一起涉网络犯罪案件，逮捕6名韩国籍嫌疑人，查获大量电子设备及现金。值得注意的是，案件线索源于一名跑腿骑手的警觉。

title: Nigerian, Swiss suspects arrested in cocaine network bust
desc: Nigerian, Swiss suspects arrested in cocaine network bust Legacy Police arrested four suspects, including Nigerian and Swiss nationals , in raids targeting a cocaine network after a months-long investigation, seizing cocaine and other assets in Bangkok and Nonthaburi.

title: Cannabis shop owner shot dead by police officer in Pattaya
desc: A police officer was arrested after allegedly shooting a cannabis shop owner dead in a Songkran shooting on Pattaya Walking Street early today, April 19.

title: Salon owner beats man to death with pestle in Nonthaburi
desc: A 58 year old Nonthaburi hair salon owner beat a young man to death with a pestle following a monetary dispute, in a killing now classified as premeditated rather than self-defence.

title: Lisa亮相科切拉：全息舞台+新歌首秀引爆全球！
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 近日，泰国籍艺人Lisa再度亮相科切拉音乐节舞台，以极具未来感的舞台制作与表演引发全球热议，成为社交媒体话题中心。

title: 曼谷国际车展订单创新高，中国品牌占近七成！
desc: 泰国汇商银行经济与商业研究中心SCB EIC发布报告指出，第47届曼谷国际车展累计汽车订单超过13万辆，中国汽车品牌在本届车展中占据约65%的订单份额。

title: Transgender woman falls to death at Bangkok luxury condo
desc: An unidentified transgender woman fell to her death from a luxury condominium in the Kaset-Nawamin area of Bangkok on Friday night.

title: Chiang Mai doctor reports severe lung cases tied to PM2.5
desc: A doctor at Chiang Mai University said on April 17 that PM2.5 and toxic smoke in northern Thailand were linked to two serious lung cases.

title: Consumer council calls for 20-baht Green Line fare
desc: Consumer council calls for 20-baht Green Line fare Legacy The Thailand Consumers Council (TCC) is pushing for a fixed 20-baht fare on Bangkok’s Green Line electric railway , alongside a broader restructuring of mass transit pricing aimed at boosting ridership by 50% and cutting household living costs. TCC secretary-general Saree Aongsomwang made the call during a press briefing titled “Green Line After 2029: Study Shows 20-Baht Fare Feasible,” arguing that urban rail should be treated as a public service rather than a private commodity. She said affordable transport is essent

title: 曼谷一豪宅深夜失火，致2死2伤
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月19日凌晨，泰国曼谷邦纳警局接到报警称辖区内一高档住宅小区发生火灾。警方随即联同素坤逸消防与救援人员赶赴现场。 据悉，起火建筑为一栋四层联排别墅，火势主要集中在二至三层并迅速蔓延。消防人员持续灭火30分钟，成功将火势控制住，避免波及周边房屋。 此次事故造成两人死亡，分别为一名约40岁女性及一名70岁德国籍女性，均因吸入浓烟身亡。另有两人受伤，包括一名约50岁德国籍男子，全身多处烧伤，已被送往医院治疗；一名约20岁德国籍男子，因吸入浓烟出现不适，被送医救治。 目前，警方正对火灾原因展开进一步调查。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：khaosod） The post 曼谷一豪宅深夜失火，致2死2伤 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 热爆了！曼谷发布高温警报，体感温度已达“危险级”！
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月19日，泰国曼谷市政府环境部门发布高温预警称，当日体感温度指数已升至“危险级”，提醒广大市民及游客密切关注自身健康状况。 曼谷市政府环境部门通过官方平台通报指出，4月19日当日体感温度指数已处于“危险级”。一般人群应留意自身身体状况变化，老年人、儿童及慢性病患者等高风险群体需密切关注，如出现异常症状，应尽快就医。 环境部门同时建议，民众可通过空气质量监测网站或应用程序持续关注空气质量及相关环境因素，以便及时采取防护措施，降低高温恶劣天气带来的不利影响。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：thairath） The post 热爆了！曼谷发布高温警报，体感温度已达“危险级”！ appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 阿努廷将率内阁多部门赴清迈，紧急应对山火与PM2.5危机
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月19日，泰国总理府发言人拉差妲透露，在完成2027财年预算政策会议相关行程后，总理兼内政部长阿努廷将率多名内阁部长及有关部门官员一同前往清迈，督导解决持续影响北部地区的山火、烟霾及PM2.5粉尘问题。 此次随行人员包括副总理颂萨、副总理兼商业部长素帕琪、国防部长阿杜、农业部长素利亚、数字经济与社会部长猜差诺、自然资源与环境部长素查、卫生部长帕塔纳以及内政部副部长杰萨等。 行程期间，政府高层将与国家防灾减灾指挥中心召开会议，跟进北部上部地区灾情及治理进展，发布更具前瞻性的政策与指示，推动问题取得实质性改善。重点措施包括规范高地农业活动、研究限制涉及焚烧生产的农产品进口、加强民众健康防护以及加大对山火防控工作的支持力度。 此外，总理一行还将实地了解灾情发展，慰问一线工作人员。政府强调，将从环境、农业、旅游及公共卫生等多维度统筹推进治理，在缓解当前影响的同时，推动问题实现长期、可持续解决。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：matichon） The post 阿努廷将率内阁多部门赴清迈，紧急应对山火与PM2.5危机 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 芭提雅醉酒警员当街开枪，大麻店主劝阻反遭射杀
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 近日，泰国芭提雅南部步行街发生一起枪击命案。一名警员在醉酒状态下开枪，不仅向附近的娱乐场所开枪，还将枪口对准了前来劝阻的大麻店老板，致其伤重不治。 现场监控画面显示，41岁的大麻店老板帕塔通走近一名身穿黑衣、在店后饮酒的便衣警员。该警员当时明显处于醉酒状态，此前已掏出手枪向附近一处娱乐场所开枪，所幸未造成人员伤亡。 帕塔通见状上前劝阻，以合十礼请求对方收起枪支。然而，该警员却突然开枪，连射两发子弹击中帕塔通。中枪后，帕塔通仍奋力夺下对方手枪，但随后意识到自己受伤，逐渐体力不支倒地，面部撞向墙壁。救援人员赶到现场全力施救，但最终未能挽回其生命。 事发后，该警员拿走死者手机并将其抛入海中，疑似试图销毁证据。周边民众合力将其控制，当时其面部带血，神志不清。 初步调查显示，涉事警员当时处于严重醉酒状态，供述混乱，否认曾开枪。目前，警方已将其拘留，案件仍在进一步调查中，后续将依法处理。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan；来源：thaich8news） The post 芭提雅醉酒警员当街开枪，大麻店主劝阻反遭射杀 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .
